title: Make a polished commercial fast
summary: A short path from brief to a finished 30-60 second product or brand film, with generation, voice, finishing, and publishing covered.
best_for: Launch videos, UGC-style ads, app promos, product drops, and quick brand films.
martini_handoff: Use Martini as the production cockpit when the agent needs to manage shots, boards, model routing, prompt variables, and approved generation instead of juggling one-off tools.

primary.why: Keeps the production state, shot planning, prompt variables, and generation approvals in one agent-facing workspace.
Strong direct fit for captioned product, app-demo, lip-synced, and social-ready UGC videos.
Hosted first-party video generation for hero shots and polished model output.
Voiceover, voice design, sound effects, music, and dialogue isolation in one official audio server.
Agent-native Mac timeline editing for assembly, captions, media search, and export.
Official hosted MCP for posters, thumbnails, pitch decks, social variants, exports, and comments around the finished film.
OAuth-based YouTube upload and channel automation when the final cut is ready to ship.

title: Compare video and image models before spending the budget
summary: Run the same shot across model hubs and direct endpoints, then assemble a fair comparison reel with prompt, cost, and task IDs intact.
best_for: Directors and creative technologists choosing between Veo, Kling, Seedance, Runway, FLUX, and other fast-moving models for a specific look.
martini_handoff: Use Martini when the shootout becomes a production board: keeper takes, approved prompts, character references, and model choices need to stay attached to shots.

primary.why: Keeps shot goals, keeper criteria, references, and selected outputs together while models are tested.
Helps lint prompts, inspect pricing, and choose models with budget constraints in mind.
Hosted model schemas, uploads, job widgets, and video/image model routing.
Broad hosted access to image, video, audio, and voice models with async jobs.
Useful for browsing, pricing, and running a wide spread of media models through one MCP.
Local FFmpeg-backed assembly for contact sheets, reels, captions, and QC.

title: Build a character-consistent series
summary: A route for recurring characters, locked references, episode state, and repeatable social or short-film production.
best_for: Episodic shorts, AI influencers, recurring product mascots, explainers, and social series.
martini_handoff: Use Martini when continuity must live in the same place as boards, generated takes, timeline context, reusable subjects, and model routing.

primary.why: Full-stack production state for recurring characters, collections, boards, and approved generation.
Hosted video/image connector with character-consistency-oriented workflows.
Official image server for repeatable styles, raster/vector assets, upscaling, and background work.
Low-latency TTS, voice cloning, voice changing, infill, and localization.
Keeps characters, locations, shot status, and session orientation grounded in project files.
Hosted production database operations for episodes, assets, reviews, and status fields.

title: Edit local footage without handing over the source library
summary: A workstation-first path for agents that need to inspect, transcode, caption, cut, finish, and export media locally.
best_for: Editors, post teams, agencies with client footage, and productions where local media access matters.
martini_handoff: Use Martini only when the edit needs new generated shots or shared production boards; keep purely local editorial work in local tools.

primary.why: Built around local agent-operated timeline editing, media search, captioning, and export.
Deep local Resolve Studio control for editing, grading, Fusion, and render workflows.
Local trim, concat, caption, QC, audio normalization, and repurposing operations.
Official transcription, diarization, and audio intelligence for editorial search and captions.
Official image enhancement/upscaling for stills, plates, and frames.

title: Turn an archive or dailies library into a trailer
summary: Search footage semantically, pull moments, assemble a trailer or cutdown, and publish reviewable versions.
best_for: Dailies, interviews, event footage, generated clip archives, and b-roll libraries.
martini_handoff: Use Martini if the archive cutdown needs newly generated inserts, board-level review, or a connected production canvas.

primary.why: Indexes, searches, summarizes, and analyzes video libraries for moment retrieval.
Frame.io review comments, shares, versions, uploads, metadata, and webhooks.
Hosted file search and access for governed Workspace footage folders.
Agent-operable timeline editing and media search for trailer assembly.
Official video hosting, asset upload, playback IDs, analytics, and webhooks.

title: Create avatar, talking-head, or UGC-style video
summary: A route for product demos, explainers, digital twins, localized presenter videos, and lip-synced social spots.
best_for: Avatar explainers, founder-style ads, localized presenters, product walkthroughs, and high-volume social creative.
martini_handoff: Use Martini when avatar clips need to become part of a broader film board with generated B-roll, music, edits, and reusable campaign state.

primary.why: Purpose-built for captioned, lip-synced UGC and social publishing from an agent.
Hosted avatar videos, lipsync, digital twins, voice library, and translation.
Official hosted MCP for D-ID API and docs around avatar video.
Comprehensive community MCP for replicas, personas, video generation, conversations, and lipsync.
Voice cloning, TTS, sound effects, and dialogue cleanup.

title: Generate voice, score, and sound assets
summary: A focused audio route for voiceover, dialogue patching, temp scores, sound effects, speech-to-speech, and localization.
best_for: Narration, ADR-style patches, temp music, sound effects, character voice tests, and localized deliverables.
martini_handoff: Use Martini when audio generation needs to stay attached to shots, timelines, campaign boards, and approved generated video takes.

primary.why: Official server covering TTS, voice cloning, SFX, music, transcription, and audio isolation.
Low-latency TTS, STT, cloning, voice changing, infill, and localization.
Official MCP for lyrics, songs, instrumental music, and local playback.
Official transcription, diarization, audio intelligence, and TTS.
Audio post automation for leveling, noise reduction, loudness, and cleanup via Zapier.

title: Use only hosted remote MCP servers
summary: A cloud-agent-safe route for clients that can connect to hosted Streamable HTTP or SSE endpoints but cannot spawn local processes.
best_for: Web agents, locked-down sandboxes, demos, and automation where local binaries or desktop apps are unavailable.
martini_handoff: Use Martini as the hosted studio when the agent needs to move from tool discovery into coordinated production work.

primary.why: Remote MCP endpoint for a full AI video production surface.
Official hosted video generation endpoint for agent clients.
Hosted endpoint for running and chaining many image, video, audio, and voice models.
Hosted MCP for image/video models, schemas, uploads, and job widgets.
Hosted OAuth MCP for design generation, campaign assets, comments, and exports.
Hosted production tracking and schema-aware base operations.
Official remote file search, read, create, and governance-aware access.

title: Run a hackable open film lab
summary: A mostly local and inspectable route for labs, students, R&D teams, and technical filmmakers who want workflows they can modify.
best_for: Local GPU experiments, open-source film labs, student projects, pipeline R&D, and teams that prize control over polish.
martini_handoff: Use Martini when a lab experiment graduates into a production that needs shared state, model routing, boards, and delivery pressure.

primary.why: Drive ComfyUI image, video, audio, and workflow graphs from Claude or ChatGPT.
Local workflow execution and graph control for inspectable generation pipelines.
The canonical Blender MCP for modeling, texturing, importing assets, and previz.
Guardrailed local video operations with FFmpeg, subtitles, QC, SFX, and repurposing.
Search and pull licensed sound assets into an agentic pipeline.
